candidates.evidence_quote: revenues finishing towards the higher end of our guidance and profitability surpassing expectations. This resulted in a 27% year over year increase in adjusted earnings per share
Total revenues of $927 million finished towards the high end of the range we provided.
we are updating our total revenue outlook to align more closely with the lower end of previous expectations
we continue to expect full year adjusted net income margin to be approximately 4.75% at the sales estimate provided
Comparable sales increased 1% versus the prior year, supported by 22% off-premise sales mix
First quarter annualized AUVs for North Italia increased 1% to $7.75 million. Comparable sales declined 1%
Flower Child continues on a strong upward trajectory. with comparable sales increasing by 5%, significantly outperforming the Black Box Fast Casual Dining Index
we were at about 4% effective pricing. Traffic was a negative 1.2, and so mix was the difference on that
We will evaluate pricing at each of those turns based on all of the factors that we know at that point in time
we put a lot of newer items with lower price points on. We actually took some higher price point items off
supported by 22% off-premise sales mix
we continue to evaluate the potential impact of the tariffs along with the business levers we control to mitigate defects
cost of sales decreased 100 basis points, primarily driven by favorable commodity costs
We are modeling net total labor inflation of low to mid single digits when factoring in the latest trends in wage rates and minimum wage increases
factoring in the latest trends in wage rates and minimum wage increases, as well as other components of labor
Labor, as a percent of sales, declined 30 basis points, primarily driven by the continued improvement in retention, supporting labor productivity gains and wage leverage
we saw further improvement in our already industry-leading manager and staff retention
the Cheesecake Factory four-wall restaurant margins increased to 17.4%, up 140 basis points from the first quarter in 2024
we continue to expect to open as many as 25 new restaurants in 2025
Pre-opening costs were $8.1 million in the quarter compared to $5.9 million in the prior year period
we completed approximately $141.4 million in share repurchases and returned $12.5 million to shareholders via our dividend
we strengthened our liquidity position and balance sheet through the issuance of $575 million of 2% convertible notes due 2030
we recorded a pre-tax net expense of $17.3 million related to loss on extinguishment of debt associated with the partial redemption of our convertible senior notes due 2026
with the impact of the Los Angeles fires weighing more heavily on performance due to the concept's smaller comp base
we're talking about unprecedented weather, the fires in Los Angeles, the holiday shifts
anticipated impacts associated with holiday shifts
in light of recent economic growth and real disposable income forecasts, which have been revised downward by approximately 1%
that aligns with our overall perspective of where GDP and disposable income is likely to fall out
there's a lot of pull forward of spending into March from consumers
Member acquisition continues to exceed our expectations, and guest feedback remains overwhelmingly positive
first quarter guest satisfaction scores improved both sequentially and year over year
our latest new menu change, which featured more than 20 new items across a broad range of contemporary cuisines and categories, garnered substantial media coverage
Other operating expenses increased 40 basis points, as expected, primarily driven by timing of marketing and rewards costs
beverage alcohol is still a little bit of a headwind as consumers adjust, and maybe it's a little bit of a secular thing
We had mentioned on the last call that we had a mid-Q2 cheesecake closure, and the location is Seattle
and external bakery sales were $12.7 million
with annualized unit volumes exceeding 12.5 million
CapEx totaled approximately $43 million during the first quarter for new unit development and maintenance

notes: Chunk contains a single event: transcript CAKE_2025-04-30T17.00 (Q1 FY2025 earnings call), part 1 of 2; names coined only from visible part. fiscal_kpis is null for this chunk (KPIs live on chunk_001).

Cheesecake Factory brand-level comps were mapped to company-wide same_store_sales because the brand name equals the company legal name (banned inside driver_name); North Italia and Flower Child brand metrics kept as separate brand-level drivers per R9.

Skipped as vague/minor/folded (8): 'increasingly competitive landscape' (bare category); 'broader macroeconomic environment has become more uncertain' (bare macro); 'food efficiencies' (vague, folded into commodity_costs/labor_productivity); routine dividend mention (no causal narrative beyond share_repurchases quote); G&A decline from lower professional fees (minor line item); 9-10% tax-rate modeling assumption; facility-related/utility cost creep (minor); imported small wares/to-go packaging exposure (folded into tariff_policy).
